summary: Azure ExpressRoute extends on-premises networks into Microsoft cloud over a private connection through a third-party connectivity provider, never traversing the public internet. It offers higher reliability, lower latency, and better security than VPN Gateway but at greater cost. It is suited for mission-critical workloads requiring consistent, high-speed connectivity.

key_concepts.explanation: ExpressRoute traffic travels over a dedicated private link through a connectivity provider, not the public internet, giving more predictable performance and higher security.
A third-party network provider facilitates the physical private connection between your on-premises network and Microsoft's network edge.
ExpressRoute uses Border Gateway Protocol to exchange routing information between your on-premises network and Azure, enabling dynamic route management.
An add-on feature that connects multiple on-premises sites to each other through the Microsoft backbone, using ExpressRoute circuits as the transport.
Two ExpressRoute circuits can be provisioned to eliminate single points of failure and achieve high availability for the private connection.

exam_focus_points: ExpressRoute does NOT use the public internet — traffic is always private through a provider
Connectivity requires a third-party connectivity provider, not a direct Microsoft link
Supports BGP routing protocol for dynamic route exchange
Two circuits can be provisioned for high availability and redundancy
ExpressRoute Global Reach connects on-premises networks to each other via Microsoft's backbone
Speed range is 50 Mbps to 100 Gbps
More expensive than VPN Gateway but provides better reliability, latency, and security guarantees
Preferred for mission-critical workloads needing consistent performance
common_mistakes: Confusing ExpressRoute with VPN Gateway — VPN Gateway encrypts traffic over the public internet; ExpressRoute avoids the public internet entirely
Assuming ExpressRoute connects directly to Microsoft without a third-party provider — a connectivity provider is always required
Thinking ExpressRoute and VPN Gateway are interchangeable for cost-sensitive scenarios — ExpressRoute is significantly more expensive
Forgetting that Global Reach is a separate add-on feature and not included by default with an ExpressRoute circuit
Assuming a single ExpressRoute circuit provides high availability — two circuits must be explicitly provisioned for HA
